assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response does not mention checking for deprecated APIs or API version changes, which is crucial when troubleshooting post-upgrade issues

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: The response mentions checking for 'custom admission webhooks that might be timing out' in the Quick Resolution Checklist

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: The response includes checking resource limits/requests with specific kubectl commands and mentions increasing API server resources if needed

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check API server pods, etcd pods, and look for restart loops in kube-system namespace

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: The response includes the specific command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_duration_seconds' in the monitoring section

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention NetworkPolicy or service mesh components that may need updating for version compatibility

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check events with 'kubectl get events' and checking for pending pods which would reveal scheduling issues

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention checking HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) behavior changes in the new version
